meta.title: MB3R Lab | Model-based resilience from service-topology models
meta.description: Model-based resilience tooling: Bering discovers and publishes topology artifacts; Sheaft simulates failures, evaluates policy gates, and monitors posture over time.
meta.ogTitle: MB3R Lab | Model-based resilience from service-topology models
meta.ogDescription: Bering discovers and publishes model artifacts. Sheaft consumes them for resilience simulation, policy evaluation, and posture monitoring.
meta.twitterTitle: MB3R Lab | Model-based resilience from service-topology models
meta.twitterDescription: Model-based resilience for engineering and observability workflows.

hero.title: Model-based resilience from service-topology models
hero.lead: Bering discovers service topology and endpoint contracts, then publishes stable model artifacts. Sheaft consumes those artifacts to simulate failures, evaluate policy gates, and track posture over time.
hero.note: This is a model-based signal: fast, repeatable, and designed to complement live experiments.

panel.beringText: Discovery and publishing layer for topology and endpoint contracts.
panel.sheaftText: Resilience posture engine for simulation, gating, and continuous monitoring.

how.title: How it works
how.intro: The workflow is intentionally split: discovery upstream, evaluation downstream.

discover.title: Bering discovers topology and endpoint contracts from traces / OTLP.
discover.text: Batch discovery works from trace files or topology inputs. Runtime mode accepts OTLP/HTTP and optional OTLP/gRPC.

publish.title: Bering publishes stable model and snapshot artifacts.
publish.text: Artifacts become explicit handoff contracts for downstream tools and CI workflows.

evaluate.title: Sheaft consumes artifacts, simulates scenarios, evaluates gate policy, and can monitor posture continuously.
evaluate.text: Use batch mode for pull requests and release checks, then switch to watch/serve mode for posture tracking.

chaos.title: Why not chaos-first
chaos.intro: Live chaos engineering remains valuable. The point is sequencing, not replacement.
points: Live experiments are expensive to run frequently across many services and release branches.
A model-based gate is cheaper and can run regularly before release.
Use model-based results to narrow and prioritize where live experiments will matter most.
This adds a pre-release layer; it does not claim to replace production validation.

bering.title: Bering: discovery and publishing layer
bering.summary: Bering is responsible for producing topology/contracts artifacts and publishing them as stable inputs.
points: Discovers service topology from traces, OTLP streams, or explicit topology input.
Builds endpoint contract and dependency artifacts for downstream consumers.
Supports deterministic batch mode and long-running runtime mode.
Does not own simulation, gating policy, or chaos execution.

sheaft.title: Sheaft: simulation, gating, and posture monitoring
sheaft.summary: Sheaft stays downstream of discovery and evaluates resilience posture from upstream model artifacts.
points: Consumes published model/snapshot artifacts from Bering or compatible producers.
Runs simulation-based resilience analysis across CI/CD and adjacent engineering workflows.
Evaluates gate policy for release decisions.
Can run as a long-lived service for posture history, diffs, and metrics.

audience.title: Who it is for
audience.intro: Teams that need a practical resilience signal before release, not a once-a-quarter exercise.

core.title: Core fit
points: Platform, SRE, and DevOps teams responsible for delivery risk.
Microservice teams already collecting traces/OTLP or topology artifacts.
Organizations that want a low-cost pre-release resilience checkpoint.

smb.title: SMB / lean teams
points: A cheaper entry path into resilience practice.
Run small, frequent checks in CI before spending on heavy live tests.

enterprise.title: Larger organizations
points: Prioritize where costly live chaos experiments should run first.
Standardize artifact handoff between discovery and gate evaluation teams.

steps.title: Install Bering and generate a first model artifact
steps.text: Use the install guide, then run discovery on sample traces or your own input.

steps.title: Publish or export the resulting model/snapshot artifact
steps.text: Use sample folders to understand expected artifact shape and handoff format.

steps.title: Run Sheaft simulation and CI gate on that artifact
steps.text: Start with the CI gate doc and the example CI assets.

steps.title: Move to continuous posture monitoring when needed
steps.text: When batch checks are stable, enable long-running posture tracking.

observability.title: Resilience posture in observability mode
observability.intro: After CI gate is in place, you can run Sheaft continuously to track posture drift instead of checking only at release time.

steps.title: Bering keeps publishing rolling snapshots
steps.text: In runtime mode, Bering emits model/snapshot artifacts per time window from OTLP and trace input.

steps.title: Sheaft serve/watch consumes each new artifact
steps.text: Every new artifact is re-evaluated against analysis rules and gate policy without waiting for the next release.

steps.title: Posture history and diffs show what changed
steps.text: Teams can inspect when posture moved, what dependencies shifted, and which policies flipped state.

steps.title: Use the signal for prioritization
steps.text: Prioritize incidents, hardening work, and expensive live chaos runs using the latest posture trend.

outputs.title: What teams get in practice
points: Current posture status and when it last changed.
Diff/history context for topology and policy outcomes.
A cheaper continuous resilience signal between live experiments.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.intro: Planned product milestones and research-to-product transition phases.
roadmap.imageAlt: MB3R Lab roadmap by phases and milestones

publications.title: Publications and preprints
publications.intro: Core papers behind the tooling and workflow.

emergenceAsCode.title: Emergence-as-Code for Self-Governing Reliable Systems
emergenceAsCode.text: Journey-level reliability governance with explicit intent, evidence, and control policies.

rasmussenSimplex.title: Toward Operationalizing Rasmussen: Drift Observability on the Simplex for Evolving Systems
rasmussenSimplex.text: A simplex-based way to monitor drift and boundary proximity in evolving systems.

asyncSemantics.title: Evaluating Asynchronous Semantics in Trace-Discovered Resilience Models
asyncSemantics.text: Validation on OpenTelemetry Demo with asynchronous semantics and live-chaos comparison.

uncertainty.title: Measuring Uncertainty in Transformer Circuits with Effective Information Consistency
uncertainty.text: A white-box metric for localizing uncertainty mechanisms in transformer circuits.

modelDiscovery.title: Model Discovery and Graph Simulation: A Lightweight Gateway to Chaos Engineering
modelDiscovery.text: Offline resilience estimation from discovered dependency graphs.

sheaf.title: Sheaf-Theoretic Causal Emergence for Resilience Analysis in Distributed Systems
sheaf.text: Foundational model for multi-level causal resilience analysis.

research.title: Built from research, packaged for engineering
research.text: The approach is grounded in resilience research, but this site focuses on practical engineering workflow: discover artifacts upstream, gate posture downstream.
